The substring() method in Java can take an int index and return the string starting at that index until the end. If you pass a negative index, it starts from the end of the string.

Wait, so for example, if `convId` is "GMT+05:00", using `substring(3)` would give "T+05:00". So for "GMT+05:00" which has length 9, substring(-3) would give the last three characters, which is ":00". That doesn't make sense because we want to extract the offset part after GMT.
